text: The Latin version does not make clear, what is evidently intended, that the men of Benjamin (to whose country the prophet belonged) are invited to escape from Jerusalem, and hold their own in the hill-country of the south.

The text here apparently says, ‘They are confounded, because they have done abominably; or rather they are not confounded, they do not know how to blush’. Probably the verb ‘to be confounded’ is used in two different senses, (i) to be ignominiously disappointed of one’s aim, (ii) to express shame, own oneself in the wrong.
According to the Hebrew text, ‘bow and javelin’.
